operator_request: Activate and implement WP-FF-005 using subagents for implementation assistance and independent multi-lens adversarial review of the implementation, then remediate and harden; reuse the research already completed for this work.

research_reopen_triggers: A selected dependency or source version has changed since the recorded basis.
A concrete implementation decision lacks evidence in the existing design, peer-review, WP-003, or WP-004 sources.
The Operator explicitly requests additional research.
existing_research_sources: The current technical design reference set and peer-review corpus
The validated WP-003 build, architecture, failure-boundary, and proof implementation
The validated WP-004 pinned compatibility inventory, seven-plane corpus, and adversarial remediation evidence
The WP-005 stub implementation brief, reuse ladder, risk scenarios, and verification matrix
selected_approach: Keep versioned wire DTOs data-only and separate from validated pure domain/state types.
Use explicit schema identity and major/minor compatibility ranges with bounded JSON inputs, stable tagged variants, typed errors, and checked conversions.
Use one bounded framing/conformance model for process, plugin, JavaScript-worker, and diagnostic envelopes without runtime handles in contract crates.
Implement state transitions as pure state-plus-event calculations returning new state and explicit effect intents.
Implement atomic resource vectors and byte credits with checked arithmetic, exact grant identity, all-or-nothing admission, bounded queues, cancellation, and deterministic replay traces.
Classify this packet as Phase 0 prerequisite evidence with zero product/runtime/phase progress until a shipped consumer re-proves it through FF-GATE-RUNTIME-001.
rejected_options: Repeating or adversarially reviewing the completed research basis without a named stale or missing-evidence trigger.

Using untagged public protocol enums, unbounded extension maps, round-trip-only compatibility proof, wrapping or saturating resource arithmetic, independent resource semaphores, runtime handles in contract crates, or tests as product-runtime proof.
Adding a new serialization format, runtime, native dependency, or concrete adapter under this prerequisite packet.
Expanding WP-005 into the blocked Phase 1 production slice merely to satisfy a runtime-progress claim.
reuse_opportunities: Reuse the locked Cargo workspace, strict Serde parsing, report writer, bounded process runner, policy parser, negative-fixture harness, and canonical gates from WP-003.

Reuse WP-004 stable IDs, bounded JSON, deterministic fixtures, content digests, seven compatibility planes, and source-graph/migration/failure/archive observations as oracle evidence rather than normative product types.
Reuse standard checked arithmetic and deterministic collections before adding any dependency.
basis.validation_plan: Run format, warnings-denied Clippy, all workspace tests, doctests/docs, architecture and dependency gates, the implemented deep contract/model gate, compatibility fixture checks, direct boundary and malformed-input probes, deterministic replay, counterfactual mutations, then independent implementation reviews through contract, serialization/security, state/resource/durability, and integration/gate lenses; remediate every confirmed finding and rerun the complete matrix.

assumptions: WP-003 and WP-004 validated outputs remain the current reusable prerequisite foundations.
Phase 0B contracts may close only as explicitly classified prerequisite evidence and do not constitute product progress.
Compatibility-facing semantics not proven by WP-004 remain typed provisional or unsupported rather than guessed.
non_goals: Concrete networking, extraction, storage, FFmpeg, JavaScript, plugin, scheduler, watcher, packaging, or release behavior.
A product capability, runtime-completion, replacement, parity, performance, or product-phase claim.
Additional research without a recorded reopen trigger.

invariants: monotonic sequence and valid prior hash
transition only after named durability prerequisites

minimum_controls: Every public contract has a stable identity, version policy, bounds, typed errors, fixtures, and inventory row.
Every state transition and durable prefix has executable success, failure, cancellation, restart, and counterfactual proof.
Every resource dimension uses checked arithmetic and all-or-nothing admission with exact release identity.
No contract crate contains runtime handles or depends outward on adapters, watcher, governance, or build tooling.
No prerequisite PASS is reported as product, capability, runtime, packaging, release, or phase progress.
plausible_failures: A current/current round trip passes while prior readers reject or reinterpret a field.
An unknown mandatory message kind or incompatible major version is silently accepted.
A declared frame length causes allocation before the configured maximum is checked.
Cancellation or an invalid transition leaks a grant or advances a durable prefix.
Independent resource checks partially acquire capacity and deadlock or exceed a coupled bound.
A renamed output is reported successful before archive reconciliation.
Green tests or a generated report are misrepresented as shipped runtime behavior.

execution_decomposition: Commit activation, prerequisite classification, completed-research reuse guard, and exact recovery checkpoint.
Generalize architecture and runtime gates for declared prerequisite product packages without weakening runtime truth.
Implement versioned contracts and diagnostic envelopes with canonical fixtures and inventory.
Implement pure transition, resource, byte-credit, durability, and recovery models with deterministic proofs.
Implement the triggered deep gate and negative/counterfactual fixtures.
Update the model manual and complete packet evidence.
Run independent multi-lens adversarial implementation reviews, remediate findings, and rerun all proof.
